steps.title: Apply the circumference formula
steps.content: The circumference of a circle is $C=\pi d$, where $d$ is the diameter. Substitute the given $C=42\pi$: $$42\pi=\pi d.$$

steps.title: Solve for diameter/radius
steps.content: Divide both sides by $\pi$: $$d=42.$$ The answer is 42.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: As a final check, make sure the computed value answers exactly what the question asks for. The work above gives $42$; substituting or interpreting it in the original condition satisfies the requirement, so $42$ is the value to enter.
